chunks.text: (In 0.12.4, `root()` in `.jac` source still worked with warning W0062; it has since been removed and is now error **E0049**.)

meta.section: 1. `root` Is a Reserved Keyword Again (`SpecialVarRef`)

chunks.text: **In library mode (Python):**

meta.section: 1. `root` Is a Reserved Keyword Again (`SpecialVarRef`)

chunks.text:
```python
from jaclang.lib import root, connect

# root() is the canonical call form in Python
connect(left=root(), right=Item())
```

meta.section: 1. `root` Is a Reserved Keyword Again (`SpecialVarRef`)
1. `root` Is a Reserved Keyword Again (`SpecialVarRef`)

chunks.text: ### Version 0.12.2

meta.section: Version 0.12.2

chunks.text:
#### 1. Filter Comprehension Syntax Changed from `(?:...)` to `[?:...]`

The parenthesized filter comprehension syntax `(?:Type)` and `(?:Type, condition)` was deprecated in favor of bracket syntax `[?:Type]` and `[?:Type, condition]`. In 0.12.2 the old syntax still parsed with deprecation warning **W0061**; it has since been removed outright and is now error **E0048**. The formatter (`jac fmt`) automatically converts old syntax to new.

meta.section: 1. Filter Comprehension Syntax Changed from `(?:...)` to `[?:...]`

chunks.text: **Before:**

meta.section: 1. Filter Comprehension Syntax Changed from `(?:...)` to `[?:...]`
1. Filter Comprehension Syntax Changed from `(?:...)` to `[?:...]`

chunks.text: **After:**

meta.section: 1. Filter Comprehension Syntax Changed from `(?:...)` to `[?:...]`
1. Filter Comprehension Syntax Changed from `(?:...)` to `[?:...]`

chunks.text: **Why:** The `[?` token sequence is unambiguous in all contexts, including nested inside edge ref chain brackets. Bracket syntax is consistent with how edge references already use `[...]`.

meta.section: 1. Filter Comprehension Syntax Changed from `(?:...)` to `[?:...]`

chunks.text: **Migration:** Run `jac fmt` on your `.jac` files to auto-convert, or manually replace `(?:` with `[?:` and `(?` with `[?` (adjusting closing `)` to `]`).

meta.section: 1. Filter Comprehension Syntax Changed from `(?:...)` to `[?:...]`
1. Filter Comprehension Syntax Changed from `(?:...)` to `[?:...]`

chunks.text: ### Version 0.11.1 / byllm 0.5.1

meta.section: Version 0.11.1 / byllm 0.5.1

chunks.text:
#### 1. LiteLLM Minimum Version Raised to 1.81.15

The `litellm` dependency for byllm has been bumped from `>=1.75.5.post1,<1.80.0` to `>=1.81.15,<1.83.0`.

meta.section: 1. LiteLLM Minimum Version Raised to 1.81.15

chunks.text: **Impact:** If your environment has other packages that pin `litellm` below `1.81.15`, dependency resolution will fail.

meta.section: 1. LiteLLM Minimum Version Raised to 1.81.15

chunks.text: **Migration:** Update `litellm` in your project or environment:

meta.section: 1. LiteLLM Minimum Version Raised to 1.81.15

chunks.text:
```bash
pip install "litellm>=1.81.15,<1.83.0"
```

meta.section: 1. LiteLLM Minimum Version Raised to 1.81.15
1. LiteLLM Minimum Version Raised to 1.81.15

chunks.text: ### Version 0.10.3

meta.section: Version 0.10.3

chunks.text:
#### 1. Test Syntax Changed from Identifiers to String Descriptions

The `test` keyword now requires a **string description** instead of an identifier name. This gives tests more readable, natural-language names with spaces, punctuation, and proper casing.

meta.section: 1. Test Syntax Changed from Identifiers to String Descriptions

chunks.text: **Before:**

meta.section: 1. Test Syntax Changed from Identifiers to String Descriptions
1. Test Syntax Changed from Identifiers to String Descriptions

chunks.text: **After:**

meta.section: 1. Test Syntax Changed from Identifiers to String Descriptions
1. Test Syntax Changed from Identifiers to String Descriptions

chunks.text: **Key Changes:**

meta.section: 1. Test Syntax Changed from Identifiers to String Descriptions

chunks.text:
- Test names must be quoted strings: `test "description" { ... }` instead of `test name { ... }`
- Spaces, punctuation, and mixed case are now allowed in test names
- The string description is displayed as-is in test output (pytest, `jac test`)
- A valid Python identifier is derived automatically for internal use (lowercased, non-alphanumeric replaced with `_`)

meta.section: 1. Test Syntax Changed from Identifiers to String Descriptions

chunks.text: **Migration:** Replace `test identifier_name {` with `test "identifier name" {` in all `.jac` files (convert underscores to spaces).

meta.section: 1. Test Syntax Changed from Identifiers to String Descriptions
1. Test Syntax Changed from Identifiers to String Descriptions

chunks.text: ### Version 0.10.2

meta.section: Version 0.10.2

chunks.text:
#### 1. CLI Dependency Commands Redesigned

The `jac add`, `jac install`, `jac remove`, and `jac update` commands were redesigned. Key behavioral changes:

meta.section: 1. CLI Dependency Commands Redesigned

chunks.text:
- `jac add` now **requires** at least one package argument (previously, calling `jac add` with no args silently fell through to install)
- `jac add` without a version spec now queries the installed version and records `~=X.Y` (previously recorded `>=0.0.0`)
- `jac install` now syncs all dependency types (pip, git, and plugin-provided like npm)
- New `jac update` command for updating dependencies to latest compatible versions
- Virtual environment is now at `.jac/venv/` instead of `.jac/packages/`

meta.section: 1. CLI Dependency Commands Redesigned
1. CLI Dependency Commands Redesigned

chunks.text: ### Version 0.10.0

meta.section: Version 0.10.0

chunks.text:
#### 1. KWESC_NAME Syntax Changed from `<>` to Backtick

Keyword-escaped names now use a backtick (`` ` ``) prefix instead of the angle-bracket (`<>`) prefix. This affects any identifier that uses a Jac keyword as a variable, field, or parameter name.

meta.section: 1. KWESC_NAME Syntax Changed from `<>` to Backtick

chunks.text: **Before:**

meta.section: 1. KWESC_NAME Syntax Changed from `<>` to Backtick
1. KWESC_NAME Syntax Changed from `<>` to Backtick

chunks.text: **After:**

meta.section: 1. KWESC_NAME Syntax Changed from `<>` to Backtick
1. KWESC_NAME Syntax Changed from `<>` to Backtick

chunks.text: **Note:** Builtin type names (`any`, `list`, `dict`, `set`, `tuple`, `type`, `bytes`, `int`, `float`, `str`, `bool`) do **not** need backtick escaping when used in expression contexts (function calls, type annotations, isinstance arguments). Backtick is only needed when using them as field, variable, or parameter names:

meta.section: 1. KWESC_NAME Syntax Changed from `<>` to Backtick
1. KWESC_NAME Syntax Changed from `<>` to Backtick

chunks.text: **Migration:** Find and replace all `<>` keyword escape prefixes with `` ` `` in your `.jac` files.

meta.section: 1. KWESC_NAME Syntax Changed from `<>` to Backtick

chunks.text:
#### 2. Backtick Type Operator Removed

The backtick (`` ` ``) type operator (`TYPE_OP`) and `TypeRef` AST node have been removed from the language. This affects two areas: walker event signatures and filter comprehensions.

meta.section: 2. Backtick Type Operator Removed

chunks.text:
##### Walker Entry/Exit Signatures

The `` `root `` syntax for referencing the Root type is replaced with `Root` (capital R).

meta.section: Walker Entry/Exit Signatures

chunks.text: **Before:**

meta.section: Walker Entry/Exit Signatures
Walker Entry/Exit Signatures

chunks.text: **After:**

meta.section: Walker Entry/Exit Signatures
Walker Entry/Exit Signatures

chunks.text: **Union types also use `Root`:**

meta.section: Walker Entry/Exit Signatures
Walker Entry/Exit Signatures
